timing: Specified after the complete valid Wave-3 audit and before any packaging use of complete Wave-4 outcomes.
requested_interpretation: The user requested that all completed waves and trials be combined and that the publication unit be a distinct concrete input (sample_id) exhibiting a same-seed paired-trial contrast, rather than a distinct canonical problem satisfying baseline 0/3 across every trial.

paired_trial_eligibility.codesteer: At one selected trial in 1--3, CodeSteer is valid, trimmed-exact, and parsed as marker_json.
paired_trial_eligibility.baselines: At that same trial and paired seed, original_plain, obfuscated_plain, and obfuscated_prompt_slice are all valid and non-exact.
paired_trial_eligibility.other_trials: Baseline conditions may be exact on other trials; baseline 0/3 is not required in this refined mode.

sequential_trigger_refinement.wave_4: Wave 4 is admissible because the complete post-Wave-3 paired-eligible distinct sample_id count is 9, below 10.
sequential_trigger_refinement.wave_5: After Wave 4 completes, skip and stop before Wave 5 when the complete post-Wave-4 paired-eligible distinct sample_id count is at least 10; include Wave 5 only when that complete count remains below 10.
